deps.title: Main existence theorem: controlled Ricci flow with surgery extends for all time
deps.label: thm:main-existence-ricci-flow-surgery

entries.label: claim:w-ab-in-ball-image
entries.title: The backward-flowed base point lies in the rescaled standard-flow ball
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.group: ch:Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body: For all $b$ sufficiently large, $w_{(a,b)}\in \psi_{(a,b)}(B(p_0,0,A)\times\{0\})$.

entries.label: rem:limit-canonical-neighborhood-is-cap
entries.title: The limiting canonical neighborhood is a cap, not a strong epsilon-neck
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
Notice that even though $x_{(a,b)}$ is the center of a strong
$\epsilon$-neck, the canonical neighborhoods of the $x_n$ constructed
in the second case are not a strong $\epsilon$-necks but rather are
$(C,\epsilon)$-caps coming from applying the flow to a
neighborhood of the surgery cap ${\mathcal C}$.

deps.title: A limiting counterexample sequence of failure points converges to a limit failure point
deps.label: lem:limit-canonical-neighborhood-fails-at-surgery-scale

entries.label: claim:limit-points-have-canonical-neighborhood
entries.title: Limit points of scalar curvature exceeding one have a doubled canonical neighborhood
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
For all $a$ sufficiently large,
every point $\tilde x\in(\widetilde{\mathcal M}_a,\widetilde G_a)$ with $R(\tilde x)>1$ and
${\bf t}(\tilde x)=0$ has a $(2C,2\epsilon)$-canonical neighborhood.

deps.title: Canonical neighborhoods for points of Omega at time T
deps.label: lem:canonical-neighborhoods-at-time-T

deps.title: A limit of strong epsilon-necks is a strong 2epsilon-neck
deps.label: claim:limit-of-necks-is-neck

deps.title: Local surgery on a delta-neck: curvature control after surgery
deps.label: thm:local-surgery

entries.label: lem:std-cap-canonical-neighborhood
entries.title: A bounded-curvature flow line into a surgery cap forces a canonical neighborhood
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
Suppose that there are $A',D',t'<\infty$ such that the following
holds for all $a$ sufficiently large.  There is a point $y_a\in
B_{\widetilde G_a}(\tilde x_a,0,A')$ and a  flow line of $\chi$
 beginning at $y_a$, defined for backward time and ending at a
 point $z_a$ in a
surgery cap ${\mathcal C}_a$ at time $-t_a$ for some $t_a\le t'$. We
denote this flow line by $y_a(t), -t_a\le t\le 0$. Furthermore,
suppose that the scalar curvature on  the  flow line from $y_a$ to
$z_a$ is bounded by $D'$. Then for all $a$ sufficiently large, $x_a$
has a strong $(C,\epsilon)$-canonical neighborhood.

deps.title: The constant overline delta_0 controlling closeness to the standard metric
deps.label: def:overline-delta-0

deps.title: Distance non-increasing under positive Ricci flow
deps.label: lem:distance-nonincreasing

deps.title: Existence, uniqueness, and properties of the standard solution
deps.label: thm:standard-solution-existence-uniqueness

deps.title: Asymptotic and curvature bounds for the standard initial metric
deps.label: lem:standard-initial-metric-asymptotic-bounds

deps.title: Trichotomy of canonical neighborhoods in the standard solution
deps.label: thm:standard-solution-trichotomy

deps.title: Gluing an evolving neck to a strong neck produces a longer strong neck
deps.label: prop:neck-gluing

deps.title: Evolution of a surgery cap stays close to the standard flow until removed
deps.label: prop:surgery-cap-evolution-alternative

entries.label: claim:curvature-bound-along-backward-flow-lines
entries.title: Uniform curvature bound along backward flow lines from a fixed ball
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
Given any $A<\infty$ there is $D(A)<\infty$ and $\delta(A)>0$ such that for all $a$
sufficiently large, $|\Rm|$ is bounded by $D(A)$ along all backward flow lines
beginning at a point of $B_{\widetilde
G_a}(\tilde x_a,0,A)$ and defined for backward time at most $\delta(A)$.

deps.title: Non-collapsing result assuming the strong canonical neighborhood assumption
deps.label: prop:kappa-limit-noncollapsing

entries.label: claim:parabolic-neighborhood-or-canonical-neighborhood
entries.title: Dichotomy: uniform backward parabolic neighborhood or a canonical neighborhood
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
After passing to a subsequence (in $a$), either:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)]
for each $A<\infty$ there are $D(A)<\infty$ and $t(A)>0$ such that for all $a$
sufficiently large $P_{\widetilde G_a}(\tilde x_a,0,A,-t(A))$ exists in
$\widetilde {\mathcal M}_a$ and $|\Rm|$ is bounded by $D(A)$ on this
backward parabolic neighborhood, or
\item[(2)] each $x_a$ has a strong
$(C,\epsilon)$-canonical neighborhood.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: A bounded-curvature flow line into a surgery cap forces a canonical neighborhood
deps.label: lem:std-cap-canonical-neighborhood

deps.title: Uniform curvature bound along backward flow lines from a fixed ball
deps.label: claim:curvature-bound-along-backward-flow-lines

entries.label: claim:uniform-t-prime-independent-of-A
entries.title: If the backward time bound cannot be made uniform in the radius, canonical neighborhoods exist
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
If the constant $t(A)>0$ cannot be chosen independent of $A$, then
after passing to a subsequence, the $x_a$ have strong
$(C,\epsilon)$-canonical neighborhoods.

deps.title: A bounded-curvature flow line into a surgery cap forces a canonical neighborhood
deps.label: lem:std-cap-canonical-neighborhood

entries.label: lem:bounded-number-of-surgeries
entries.title: The number of surgery times up to T_{i+1} is bounded
entries.chapter: Completion of the proof of Theorem~{\ref{MAIN}}
entries.body:
Given a Ricci flow with surgery $({\mathcal M},G)$ defined on $[0,T)$ with
$T\le T_{i+1}$ with surgery control parameter $\overline\delta$ a
non-increasing positive function defined on $[0,T_{i+1}]$ satisfying the
hypotheses of Theorem~\ref{MAIN} on its entire time-domain of definition, there
is a constant $N$ depending only on the volume of $(M_0,g(0))$, on $T_{i+1}$,
on $r_{i+1}$, and on $\overline\delta(T_{i+1})$ such that this Ricci flow with
surgery defined on the interval $[0,T)$ has at most $N$ surgery times.

deps.title: Asymptotic and curvature bounds for the standard initial metric
deps.label: lem:standard-initial-metric-asymptotic-bounds

deps.title: Main existence theorem: controlled Ricci flow with surgery extends for all time
deps.label: thm:main-existence-ricci-flow-surgery

entries.label: thm:finite-time-extinction-main
entries.title: Finite-time extinction for Ricci flow with surgery
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(0))$ be a compact, connected normalized Riemannian $3$-manifold.
Suppose that the fundamental group of $M$ is a free product of finite groups
and infinite cyclic groups. Then $M$ contains no $\Ar P^2$ with trivial normal
bundle.  Let $({\mathcal M},G)$ be the Ricci flow with surgery defined for all
$t\in [0,\infty)$ with $(M,g(0))$ as initial conditions given by
Theorem~\ref{MAIN}. This Ricci flow with surgery becomes extinct after a finite
time in the sense that the time-slices $M_T$ of ${\mathcal M}$ are empty for
all $T$ sufficiently large.

deps.title: Geometrization is inherited across surgery times
deps.label: cor:geometrization-inherited-across-surgery

deps.title: Finite-time extinction for Ricci flow with surgery
deps.label: thm:finite-time-extinction-main

deps.title: Main existence theorem: controlled Ricci flow with surgery extends for all time
deps.label: thm:main-existence-ricci-flow-surgery

entries.label: def:path-of-components
entries.title: Path of components
entries.body:
Let $I$ be an interval (which is allowed to be open or closed at
each end and finite or infinite at each end). By a {\em path of
components} of a Ricci flow with surgery $({\mathcal M},G)$ defined
for all $t\in I$ we mean a connected, open subset ${\mathcal
X}\subset {\bf t}^{-1}(I)$ with the property that for every $t\in I$
the intersection ${\mathcal X}(t)$ of ${\mathcal X}$ with each
time-slice $M_t$ is a connected component of $M_t$.

entries.label: lem:free-product-rank-bound
entries.title: Rank bound for free product decompositions
entries.body:
Suppose that $G$ is a finitely generated group,
say generated by $k$ elements. Let $G=G_1*\cdots*G_\ell$ be a free
product decomposition of $G$ with non-trivial free factors, i.e.,
with $G_i\not=\{1\}$ for each $i=1,\ldots,\ell$. Then $\ell\le k$.

entries.label: def:homotopically-essential-family
entries.title: Homotopically essential family of 2-spheres
entries.body:
Let $X$ be a compact $3$-manifold (possibly disconnected). An
embedded $2$-sphere in $X$ is said to be {\em homotopically
essential} if the inclusion of the $2$-sphere into $X$ is not
homotopic to a point map of the $2$-sphere to $X$. More generally,
let $F=\{\Sigma_1,\ldots,\Sigma_n\}$ be a family of disjointly
embedded $2$-spheres in $X$. We say that the family is {\em
homotopically essential} if
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(i)] each $2$-sphere in the family is homotopically essential, and
\item[(ii)] for any $1\le i<j\le n$, the inclusion of $\Sigma_i$ into $X$ is not
homotopic in $X$ to the inclusion of $\Sigma_j$ into $X$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: lem:homotopically-essential-family-finite
entries.title: Finiteness of homotopically essential families of 2-spheres
entries.body:
Let $X$ be a compact $3$-manifold (possibly disconnected). Then
there is a finite upper bound to the number of spheres in any
homotopically essential family of disjointly embedded $2$-spheres.

deps.title: Rank bound for free product decompositions
deps.label: lem:free-product-rank-bound

entries.label: def:homotopically-essential-surgery
entries.title: Homotopically essential surgery
entries.body:
Let $({\mathcal M},G)$ be a Ricci flow with surgery. We say that a
surgery along a $2$-sphere $S_0(t)$ at time $t$ in $({\mathcal
M},G)$ is a {\em homotopically essential surgery} if, for every
$t'<t$ sufficiently close to $t$,  flowing  $S_0(t)$ backwards from
time $t$ to time $t'$ results in a homotopically essential
$2$-sphere $S_0(t')$ in $M_{t'}$.

entries.label: prop:finitely-many-homotopically-essential-surgeries
entries.title: Finitely many homotopically essential surgeries
entries.body:
Let $({\mathcal M},G)$ be a Ricci flow with surgery satisfying
Assumptions (1) -- (7) in Chapter~\ref{sect:surgery}. Then there can
be only finitely many homotopically essential surgeries along
$2$-spheres in $({\mathcal M},G)$.

deps.title: Topological effect of a surgery time on the earlier time-slice
deps.label: prop:surgery-topological-type

entries.label: prop:eventual-trivial-pi2
entries.title: Eventual triviality of $\pi_2$
entries.body:
Let $({\mathcal M},G)$ be a Ricci flow with surgery satisfying the conclusion
of Theorem~\ref{MAIN}. Then there is some time $T_1<\infty$ such that every
component of $M_T$ for any $T\ge T_1$ has trivial $\pi_2$. For every $T\ge
T_1$, each component of $M_T$ either has finite fundamental group, and hence
has a homotopy $3$-sphere as universal covering, or has  contractible universal
covering.

entries.label: lem:minimal-sphere-energy-gap
entries.title: Minimal energy gap for homotopically non-trivial 2-spheres
entries.body:
Let $X$ be a compact Riemannian manifold with $\pi_2(X)\not=0$. Then
there is a positive number $e_0=e_0(X)$ with the following two
properties:
\begin{enumerate} \item Any map $f\colon S^2\to X$ with area less
than $e_0$ is homotopic to a point map. \item There is a minimal
$2$-sphere $f\colon S^2\to X$, which is a branched immersion, with
the property that the area of $f(S^2)=e_0$ and with the property
that $f$ is not homotopic to a point map.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: lem:w2-forward-difference-quotient
entries.title: Forward difference quotient for $W_2$
entries.body:
$$\frac{d}{dt}W_2(t)\le -4\pi-\frac{1}{2}R_{\min}(t)W_2(t)$$
in the sense of forward difference quotients. If $t$ is not a
surgery time, then $W_2(t)$ is continuous at $t$, and if $t$ is a
surgery time, then $$W_2(t)\le \liminf_{t'\rightarrow
t^-}W_2(t').$$

deps.title: Variation of area of a minimal 2-sphere
deps.label: claim:minimal-sphere-area-variation

deps.title: Minimal energy gap for homotopically non-trivial 2-spheres
deps.label: lem:minimal-sphere-energy-gap

entries.label: claim:sphere-area-growth-bound
entries.title: Area growth bound for a fixed 2-sphere map
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow on a compact
manifold. Suppose that $|\Ric_{g(t)}|\le D$ for all $t\in
[t_0,t_1]$ Let $f\colon S^2\to (M,g(t_0))$ be a $C^1$-map. Then
$$\Area_{g(t_1)}f(S^2)\le \Area_{g(t_0)}f(S^2)e^{4D(t_1-t_0)}.$$

entries.label: cor:sphere-theorem-no-rp2
entries.title: Sphere theorem for closed 3-manifolds without embedded $\mathbb{RP}^2$
entries.body:
Suppose that $M$ is a closed, connected $3$-manifold containing no
embedded $\Ar P^2$ with trivial normal bundle, and suppose that
$\pi_2(M)\not= 0$. Then either $M$ can be written as a connected sum
$M_1\# M_2$ where neither of the $M_i$ is homotopy equivalent to
$S^3$ or $M_1$ has a prime factor that is a $2$-sphere bundle over
$S^1$. In either case, $M$ contains an embedded $2$-sphere which is
homotopically non-trivial.

entries.label: rem:pi2-disappearing-components
entries.title: Components with non-trivial $\pi_2$ that disappear
entries.body:
Notice that it follows from the list of disappearing components that
the only ones with non-trivial $\pi_2$ are those based on the
geometry $S^2\times \Ar$; that is to say, $2$-sphere bundles over
$S^1$ and $\Ar P^3\#\Ar P^3$. Thus, once we have reached the level
$T_0$ after which all $2$-sphere surgeries are performed on
homotopically trivial $2$-spheres the only components that can have
non-trivial $\pi_2$ are components of these types. Thus, for example
if the original manifold has no $\Ar P^3$ prime factors and no
non-separating $2$-spheres, then when we reach time $T_0$ we have
done a connected sum decomposition into components each of which has
trivial $\pi_2$. Each of these components is either covered by a
contractible $3$-manifold or by a homotopy $3$-sphere, depending on
whether its fundamental group has infinite or finite order.

deps.title: Finite-time extinction for Ricci flow with surgery
deps.label: thm:finite-time-extinction-main

entries.label: claim:loop-space-pi2-pi3-iso
entries.title: $\pi_2$ of the loop space identified with $\pi_3$
entries.body:
Suppose that $\pi_2(M,x_0)=0$. Then $\pi_2(\Lambda M,*)\cong
\pi_3(M,x_0)$ and $\pi_2(\Lambda M,*)$ is identified with the free
homotopy classes of maps of $S^2$ to the component of $\Lambda M$
consisting of homotopically trivial loops.

entries.label: def:w-xi-spanning-disk-functional
entries.title: Spanning-disk energy functional $W(\xi)$
entries.body:
Fix a homotopically trivial loop $\gamma\in \Lambda M$. We set
$A(\gamma)$ equal to the infimum of the areas of any spanning disks
for $\gamma$, where by definition a spanning disk is a Lipschitz map
$D^2\to M$ whose boundary is, up to reparameterization, $\gamma$.
Notice that $A(\gamma)$ is a continuous function of $\gamma$ in
$\Lambda M$. Also, notice that $A(\gamma)$ is invariant under
reparameterization of the curve $\gamma$. Now suppose that
$\Gamma\colon S^2\to \Lambda M$ is given with the image consisting
of homotopically trivial loops.
 We define $W(\Gamma)$ to be equal to the maximum over all $c\in S^2$ of
$A(\Gamma(c))$. More generally, given a homotopy class $\xi\in \pi_2(\Lambda
M,*)$ we define $W(\xi)$ to be equal to the infimum over all (not necessarily
based) maps $\Gamma\colon S^2\to\Lambda M$ into the component of $\Lambda M$
consisting of homotopically trivial loops representing $\xi$ of $W(\Gamma)$.

entries.label: prop:w3-forward-difference-quotient
entries.title: Forward difference quotient for $W_\xi$
entries.body:
Suppose that $({\mathcal M},G)$ is a Ricci flow with surgery as in
Theorem~\ref{MAIN}. Let ${\mathcal X}$ be a path of components of ${\mathcal
M}$ defined for all $t\in[t_0,t_1]$ with $\pi_2({\mathcal X}(t_0))=0$. Suppose
that $\xi\in \pi_3( X(t_0),*)$ is a non-trivial element. Then the function
$W_\xi(t)$ satisfies the following inequality in the sense of forward
difference quotients:
$$\frac{d W_\xi(t)}{dt}\le -2\pi-\frac{1}{2}R_\mathrm{min}(t)W_\xi(t).$$ Also, for every $t\in [t_0,t_1]$ that is not a
surgery time the function $W_\xi(t)$ is continuous at $t$. Lastly,
if $t$ is a surgery time then
$$W_\xi(t)\le \liminf_{t'\rightarrow t^-}W_\xi(t').$$

deps.title: Main existence theorem: controlled Ricci flow with surgery extends for all time
deps.label: thm:main-existence-ricci-flow-surgery

entries.label: claim:w-xi-continuity-nonsurgery
entries.title: Continuity of $W_\xi$ away from surgery times
entries.body: If $t$ is not a surgery time, then $W_\xi(t)$ is continuous at $t$.

entries.label: claim:w-xi-surgery-semicontinuity
entries.title: Upper semicontinuity of $W_\xi$ at surgery times
entries.body:
Suppose that $t$ is a surgery time. Then
$$W_\xi(t)\le \mathrm{liminf}_{t'\rightarrow t^-}W_\xi(t').$$

entries.label: def:w-a-t-ode-solution
entries.title: Comparison ODE solution $w_{a,t'}$
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow on a compact
$3$-manifold. For any $a$ and any $t'\in [t_0,t_1]$ let
$w_{a,t'}(t)$ be the solution to the differential equation
\begin{equation}
\frac{dw_{a,t'}}{dt}=-2\pi-\frac{1}{2}R_{\min}(t)w_{a,t'}(t)
\end{equation}
with initial condition $w_{a,t'}(t')=a$. We also denote $w_{a,t_0}$
by $w_a$.

entries.label: prop:curve-deformation-existence
entries.title: Existence of the curve-shrinking deformation
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow on a compact
$3$-manifold.  Fix a map $\Gamma$ of $S^2$ to $\Lambda M$
 whose image consists of homotopically trivial loops and $\zeta>0$. Then there
is a continuous family $\widetilde\Gamma(t),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, of
maps $S^2\to \Lambda M$ whose image consists of homotopically
trivial loops with $[\widetilde\Gamma(t_0)]=[\Gamma]$ in
$\pi_3(M,*)$ such that for each $c\in S^2$ we have
$|A(\widetilde\Gamma(t_0)(c))-A(\Gamma(c))|<\zeta$ and furthermore,
one of the following two alternatives holds:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(i)] The length of $\widetilde\Gamma(t_1)(c)$ is less than $\zeta$.
\item[(ii)] $A(\widetilde\Gamma(t_1)(c))\le w_{A(\widetilde\Gamma(t_0)(c))}(t_1)+\zeta$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: def:a-function-integral
entries.title: Auxiliary integral $A(t)$
entries.body: Let $A(t)=\int_{t'}^t\frac{1}{2}R_{\min}(s)ds$.

entries.label: claim:w-a-t-explicit-formula
entries.title: Explicit formula for $w_{a,t'}$
entries.body:
We have
$$w_{a,t'}(t'')=\exp(-A(t''))\left(a-2\pi\int_{t'}^{t''}\mathrm{exp}(A(t))dt\right).$$ If $a'>a$, then for $t_0\le t'<t''\le t_1$,
we have
$$w_{a',t'}(t'')=w_{a,t'}(t'')+(a'-a)\exp(-A(t'')).$$

entries.label: lem:short-loops-trivial-pi3
entries.title: Families of short loops represent the trivial $\pi_3$ class
entries.body:
Given a compact Riemannian manifold $(X,g)$ with $\pi_2(X)=0$. Then
there is $\zeta>0$ such that if $\xi\in \pi_3({\mathcal X})$ is
represented by a family $\Gamma\colon S^2\to\Lambda X$ with the
property that for every $c\in S^2$ the length of the loop
$\Gamma(c)$ is less than $\zeta$, then $\xi$ is the trivial homotopy
element.

entries.label: cor:short-loop-bounds-small-disk
entries.title: Short loops bound small-area disks
entries.body:
Let $(X,g)$ be a compact Riemannian manifold. Given $\eta>0$ there is a
$0<\zeta<\eta/2$ such that any $C^1$-loop $c\colon S^1\to X$ of length less
than $\eta$ bounds a disk in $X$ of area less than $\eta$.

deps.title: Families of short loops represent the trivial $\pi_3$ class
deps.label: lem:short-loops-trivial-pi3

entries.label: claim:curve-shrinking-short-time-existence
entries.title: Short-time existence of the curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
For any immersed $C^2$-curve $c\colon S^1\to (M,g(t_0))$ there is a
curve-shrinking flow $c(x,t)$ defined for $t\in [t_0,t_1')$ for some
$t_1'>t_0$ with the property that each $c(\cdot,t)$ is an immersion.
Either the curve-shrinking flow extends to a curve-shrinking flow
that is a family of immersions defined at $t_1'$ and beyond, or
$\max_{x\in S^1}k(x,t)$ blows up as $t$ approaches $t_1'$ from
below.

entries.label: lem:embedded-curve-shrinking-area-bound
entries.title: Area bound for embedded curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
Suppose that $c\in \Lambda M$ is a homotopically trivial, embedded
$C^2$-loop. and suppose that there is a curve-shrinking flow
$c(x,t)$ defined for all $t\in [t_0,t_1]$ with each $c(\cdot,t)$
being an embedded smooth curve. Consider the function $A(t)$ which
assigns to $t$ the minimal area of a spanning disk for $c(\cdot,t)$.
Then $A(t)$ is a continuous function of $t$ and
$$\frac{dA}{dt}(t)\le -2\pi-\frac{1}{2}R_{\min}(t)A(t)$$
in the sense of forward difference quotients.

deps.title: Variation of area of a minimal 2-sphere
deps.label: claim:minimal-sphere-area-variation

entries.label: cor:embedded-curve-shrinking-area-comparison
entries.title: Area comparison for embedded curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
With notation and assumptions as above, if the curve-shrinking flow
is defined on the interval $[t^-,t^+]$ and if the curves
$c(\cdot,t)$ are embedded for all $t\in [t^-,t^+]$ then
$$A(t^+)\le \psi(t^+).$$

entries.label: lem:immersed-curve-shrinking-area-bound
entries.title: Area bound for immersed curve-shrinking flow in dimension at least 3
entries.body:
Suppose that the dimension of $M$ is at least $3$ and that
$c(\cdot,t)$ is a $C^2$-family of homotopically trivial, immersed
curves satisfying the curve-shrinking equation defined for $t^-\le
t\le t^+$. For each $t$, let $A(t)$ be the infimum of the areas of
spanning disks for  $c(\cdot,t)$. Then $A(t)$ is a continuous
function and, with $\psi$ as above, we have
$$A(t^+)\le \psi(t^+).$$

deps.title: Area bound for embedded curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: lem:embedded-curve-shrinking-area-bound

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-first-variation
entries.title: First variation formulas for curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
Assume that $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, is a Ricci flow and that
$c=c(x,t)$ is a solution to the curve-shrinking flow. We have vector
fields $X=\partial /\partial x$ and $H=\partial /\partial t$ defined
on the domain surface. We denote by $|X|_{c^*g}^2$ the function on
the domain surface whose value at $(x,t)$ is $|(X(x,t))|_{g(t)}^2$.
We define $S=|X|_{c^*g}^{-1}X$, the unit vector in the $x$-direction
measured in the evolving metric. Then,
$$\frac{\partial}{\partial t}(|X|_{c^*g}^2)(x,t)=-2\Ric_{g(t)}(X(x,t),X(x,t))-2k^2|X(x,t)|_{g(t)}^2,$$
and
$$[H,S](x,t)=\left(k^2+\Ric_{g(t)}(S(x,t),S(x,t))\right)S(x,t).$$

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-curvature-evolution
entries.title: Evolution equation for the curvature $k^2$
entries.body:
\begin{eqnarray*}
\frac{\partial }{\partial t}k^2 & = & \frac{\partial ^2}{\partial
s^2}(k^2)-2\langle(\nabla_XH)^\perp,(\nabla_SH)^\perp\rangle_{c^*g}+2k^4\\
& & -2\Ric(H,H) +4k^2\Ric(S,S)+2\Rm(H,S,H,S),\end{eqnarray*} where the superscript $\perp$ means the
image under projection to the orthogonal complement of $X$.

deps.title: First variation formulas for curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-first-variation

entries.label: claim:curve-shrinking-curvature-inequality
entries.title: Differential inequality for the curvature $k$
entries.body:
There is a constant $C_1<\infty$ depending only on an
upper bound for the norm of the sectional curvatures of the ambient
manifolds in the Ricci flow $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, such that
$$\frac{\partial}{\partial t} k\le k''+k^3+C_1k.$$

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-growth
entries.title: Growth bounds for length and total curvature
entries.body:
There is a constant $C_2<\infty$ depending only on an
upper bound for the norm of the sectional curvatures of the ambient
manifolds in the Ricci flow such that
\begin{equation}
\frac{d}{dt}L\le \int (C_2-k^2)ds\end{equation} and
$$\frac{d}{dt}\Theta\le C_2\Theta.$$

deps.title: First variation formulas for curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-first-variation

entries.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound
entries.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
entries.body:
The following holds for the constant $C_2$ as in the previous
lemma. Let $c(x,t)$ be a curve-shrinking flow, let $L(t)$ be the
total length of $c(t)$ and let $\Theta(t)$ be the total curvature of
$c(t)$. Then for any $t_0\le t'<t''\le t_1$ we have $$L(t'')\le
L(t')e^{C_2(t''-t')}$$
$$\Theta(t'')\le \Theta(t')e^{C_2(t''-t')}.$$

entries.label: claim:ramp-function-evolution
entries.title: Evolution equation for the ramp function $u$
entries.body:
$$\frac{\partial u}{\partial t}=u''+(k^2+\Ric(S,S))u\ge u''-C'u,$$
where $C'$ is an upper bound for the norm of the Ricci curvature of
$(M,g(t))$.

deps.title: First variation formulas for curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-first-variation

entries.label: def:ramp-curve
entries.title: Ramp
entries.body:
A curve $c\colon S^1\to  M\times S^1_\lambda$ is said to be a {\em
ramp} if $u$ is strictly positive.

entries.label: cor:ramp-preserved-under-flow
entries.title: Ramps remain ramps under curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
If $c(x,t),\ t_0\le t< t'_1<\infty$, is a solution of the curve
shrinking flow in $( M, g(t))\times (S^1_\lambda,ds^2)$ and if
$c(t_0)$ a ramp, then $c(t)$ is a ramp for all $t\in [t_0,t'_1)$.

deps.title: Evolution equation for the ramp function $u$
deps.label: claim:ramp-function-evolution

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-exists-for-ramps
entries.title: Curve-shrinking flow exists for all time for ramps
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow. Suppose that
$c\colon S^1\to ( M\times S^1_\lambda, g(t)\times ds^2)$ is a ramp.
Then there is a curve-shrinking flow $c(x,t)$ defined for all $t\in
[t_0,t_1]$ with $c$ as the initial condition at time $t=t_0$. The
curves $c(\cdot,t)$ are all ramps.

deps.title: Ramps remain ramps under curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: cor:ramp-preserved-under-flow

deps.title: Evolution equation for the ramp function $u$
deps.label: claim:ramp-function-evolution

deps.title: Short-time existence of the curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: claim:curve-shrinking-short-time-existence

entries.label: lem:minimal-annulus-area-growth
entries.title: Growth rate of the minimal annulus between ramps
entries.body:
Suppose that the dimension $n$ of $ M$
is at least three. Let $c_1(x,t)$ and $c_2(x,t)$ be ramp solutions
in $( M, g)\times (S^1_\lambda,ds^2)$ with the image under the
projection to $S^1_\lambda$ of each $c_i$ being of degree one. Let
$\mu(t)$ be the infimum of the areas of annuli in $( M\times
S^1_\lambda, g(t)\times ds^2)$ with boundary $c_1(x,t)\cup
c_2(x,t)$. Then $\mu(t)$ is a continuous function of $t$ and
$$\frac{d}{dt}\mu(t)\le (2n-1)\max_{x\in M}|\Rm(x,t)|\mu(t),$$
in the sense of forward difference quotients.

entries.label: cor:minimal-annulus-exponential-growth
entries.title: Exponential growth bound for the minimal annulus area
entries.body:
Given  curve-shrinking flows $c_1(\cdot,t)$ and $c_2(\cdot,t)$ for
ramps of degree one in $(\bar M,\bar g(t))\times (S^1_\lambda,ds^2)$
the minimal area of an annulus connecting $c_1(\cdot,t)$ and
$c_2(\cdot,t)$ grows at most exponentially with time with an
exponent determined by an upper bound on the sectional curvature of
the ambient flow, which in particular is independent of $\lambda$.

entries.label: lem:good-approximation-family
entries.title: Good C^2-approximation of a family of loops
entries.body:
Given a continuous map $\Gamma\colon S^2\to \Lambda M$ representing
an element of $\pi_3( M,*)$ and $0<\zeta<1$, there is a continuous
map $\widetilde \Gamma\colon S^2\to \Lambda M$ with the following
properties:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $[\widetilde \Gamma]=[\Gamma]$ in $\pi_3(M,*)$.
\item[(2)] For each $c\in S^2$ the loop $\widetilde \Gamma(c)$ is a
$C^2$-loop.
\item[(3)] For each $c\in S^2$ the length of $\widetilde \Gamma(c)$ is
within $\zeta$ of the length of $\Gamma(c)$.
\item[(4)] For each $c\in S^2$, we have $|A(\widetilde
\Gamma(c))-A(\Gamma(c))|<\zeta$.
\item[(5)] There is a constant $C_0<\infty$ depending only on $\Gamma$,
on the bounds for the norm of the Riemann curvature operator of the
ambient Ricci flow, and on $\zeta$ such that for each $c\in S^2$ and
each $\lambda\in (0,1)$ the total length and the total curvature of
the ramp $\widetilde \Gamma(c)^\lambda$ are both bounded by $C_3$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: def:n-polygonal-approximation
entries.title: Regular n-polygonal approximation
entries.body:
Let $c\colon S^1\to M$ be a $C^1$-map.  Fix a positive integer $n$.
By a {\em regular $n$-polygonal approximation} to $c$ we mean the
following. Let $\xi_n=\exp(2\pi i/n)$, and consider the points
$p_k=c(\xi^k_n)$ for $k=1,\ldots,n+1$. For each $1\le k\le n$, let
$A_k$ be a minimal geodesic in $M$ from $p_k$ to $p_{k+1}$. We
parameterize $A_k$ by the interval $[\xi_n^k,\xi_n^{k+1}]$ in the
circle at constant speed. This gives a  piecewise geodesic map
$c_n\colon S^1\to M$.

entries.label: claim:polygonal-approximation-properties
entries.title: Basic properties of n-polygonal approximations
entries.body:
Given $\zeta>0$ and a $C^1$-map $c\colon S^1\to M$ then for all $n$
sufficiently large the following hold for the $n$-polygonal
approximation $c_n$ of $c$.
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(a)] the length of $c_n$ is within $\zeta$ of the length of $c$.
\item[(b)] there is a map of the annulus $S^1\times I$ to $M$ connecting
$c_n$ to $c$ with the property that the image is piecewise smooth
and of area less than $\zeta$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: claim:polygonal-approximation-uniform
entries.title: Uniformity of the polygonal approximation over a compact family
entries.body:
Let $X\subset \Lambda M$ be a compact subset and let $\zeta>0$ be
fixed. Then there is $N$ depending only on $X$ and $\zeta$ such the
conclusion of the previous claim holds for every $c\in X$ and every
$n\ge N$.

entries.label: cor:polygonal-approximation-of-family
entries.title: Polygonal approximation of a family $\Gamma$
entries.body:
Let $\Gamma\colon S^2\to \Lambda M$ be a continuous map  with the
property that $\Gamma(c)$ is homotopically trivial for all $c\in
S^2$. Fix $\zeta>0$. For any $n$ sufficiently large denote by
$\Gamma_n$ the family of loops defined by setting $\Gamma_n(c)$
equal to the  $n$-polygonal approximation to $\Gamma(c)$. There is
$N$ such that for all $n\ge N$ we have
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $\Gamma_n$ is a continuous family of $n$-polygonal loops in
$M$.
\item[(2)] For each $c\in S^2$, the loop
$\Gamma_n(c)$ is a homotopically trivial loop in $M$ and its length
is within $\zeta$ of the length of $\Gamma(c)$.
\item[(3)] For each $c\in S^2$, we have $|A(\Gamma_n(c))-A(\Gamma(c))|<\zeta$.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: Uniformity of the polygonal approximation over a compact family
deps.label: claim:polygonal-approximation-uniform

entries.label: claim:area-forward-difference-linear-bound
entries.title: Linear bound on the forward difference quotient of area
entries.body:
There is a constant $C_4$ depending on $t_1-t_0$, on the curvature
bound of the sectional curvature of the Ricci flow $(M,g(t)),\
t_0\le t\le t_1$, on the original family $\Gamma$ and on $\zeta$
such that for any $c\in S^2$ and any $t_0\le t'<t''\le t_1$ we have
$$A(p_1\widetilde \Gamma^\lambda_c(t''))-A(p_1\widetilde
\Gamma^\lambda_c(t'))\le C_4(t''-t').$$

deps.title: Growth bounds for length and total curvature
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-growth

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-shi-estimate
entries.title: Shi-type curvature estimate for curve-shrinking flow
entries.body:
Let $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow. Then there exist
constants $\delta>0$ and $\widetilde C_i<\infty$ for
$i=0,1,2,\ldots$, depending only on $t_1-t_0$  and a bound for the
norm of the curvature of the Ricci flow, such that the following
holds. Let $c(x,t)$ be a curve-shrinking flow that is an immersion
for each $t$. Suppose that at a time $t'$ for some $0<r<1$ such that
$t'+\delta r^2<t_1$, the length of $c(\cdot,t')$ is at least $r$ and
the total curvature of $c(\cdot,t')$ on any subarc of length $r$ is
at most $\delta$. Then for every $t\in [t',t'+\delta r^2)$ the
curvature $k$ and the higher derivatives satisfy
$$k^2\le\widetilde C_0(t-t')^{-1}$$
$$|\nabla_SH|^2\le \widetilde C_1(t-t')^{-2},$$
$$|\nabla_S^iH|^2\le \widetilde C_i(t-t')^{-(i+1)}.$$

entries.label: lem:curve-deformation-single-point-estimate
entries.title: Sharper single-point estimate for the curve deformation
entries.body:
Given $\zeta>0$, there is   $1<B<\infty$, with $B>(t_1-t_0)^{-1}$,
depending only on $\Gamma$ and the curvature bounds on the ambient
Ricci flow $(M,g(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, such that the following
holds. Let $t_2=t_1-B^{-1}$. Fix $ c\in S^2$. Let $v_c$ be the
solution to Equation~(\ref{wdiffeqn}) with initial condition
$v_{c}(t_0)=A(p_1(\widetilde\Gamma(c)))$, so that in our previous
notation $v_c=w_{A(p_1(\widetilde\Gamma(c)))}$. Then for all
$\lambda>0$ sufficiently small, either
$A(p_1\widetilde\Gamma_c^\lambda(t_1))<v_c(t_1)+\zeta/2$ or the
length of $\widetilde\Gamma_c^\lambda(t)$ is less than $\zeta/2$ for
all $t\in [t_2,t_1]$.

deps.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
deps.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound

deps.title: Piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
deps.label: claim:piecewise-domination-estimate

deps.title: Area bound for immersed curve-shrinking flow in dimension at least 3
deps.label: lem:immersed-curve-shrinking-area-bound

deps.title: Short-time existence of the curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: claim:curve-shrinking-short-time-existence

deps.title: Linear bound on the forward difference quotient of area
deps.label: claim:area-forward-difference-linear-bound

deps.title: Approximate piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
deps.label: claim:approximate-piecewise-domination-estimate

deps.title: Length bound in the short-loop alternative
deps.label: rem:short-loop-length-bound

entries.label: claim:approximate-piecewise-domination-estimate
entries.title: Approximate piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
entries.body:
Fix $\zeta>0$, $A$ and $C_6, C_4$ as in the last claim, and let
$\delta'>0$ be as in the last claim. Suppose that we have $J\subset
[t_0,t_1]$ which is a finite disjoint union of intervals with
$t_1-t_0-|J|\le \delta'$.
 Then there
is $\delta''>0$ ($\delta''$ is allowed to depend on $J$) such that
the following holds. Suppose that we have a function $f\colon
[t_0,t_1]\to \Ar$ such that:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] For all $t'<t''$ in $[t_0,t_1]$ we have $f(t'')-f(t')\le
C_4(t''-t')$.
\item[(2)] For any interval $[a,b]\subset J$ we have
$f(b)\le w_{f(a),a}(b)+\delta''$.
\end{enumerate}
Then $f(t_1)\le w_{f(t_0),t_0}(t_1)+\zeta/2$.

deps.title: Piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
deps.label: claim:piecewise-domination-estimate

entries.label: claim:curve-shrinking-flow-limit
entries.title: Limit of the curve-shrinking flows
entries.body:
After passing to a subsequence of $\{\lambda_n\}$, either:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] for each $n$ sufficiently large there is $t_n\in J_B(c)$ with
the length of $\widetilde\Gamma_c^{\lambda_n}(t_n)<\delta^2B^{-1}$,
or
\item[(2)] for each component $J_i=[t_i^-,t_i^+]$ of $ J_B(c)$, after
composing $\widetilde\Gamma_c^{\lambda_n}(x,t)$ by a
reparameterization of the domain circle (fixed in $t$ but a
different reparameterization for each $n$) so that the $\widetilde
\Gamma_c^{\lambda_n}(t_i^-)$ have constant speed, there is a smooth
limiting curve-shrinking flow denoted $\widetilde\Gamma_c(t)$, for
$t\in J_i$ for the sequence $p_1\widetilde\Gamma_c^{\lambda_n}(t),\
t_i^-\le t\le t_i^+$. The limiting flow consists of immersions.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: Short-time existence of the curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: claim:curve-shrinking-short-time-existence

entries.label: rem:short-loop-length-bound
entries.title: Length bound in the short-loop alternative
entries.body:
Notice that if the first case holds then by the choice of $B$ we
have a point $t_n\in J_B(c)$ for which the length of
$\widetilde\Gamma_c^{\lambda_n}(t_n)$ is less than
$e^{-C_2(t_1-t_0)}\zeta/3$.

deps.title: Sharper single-point estimate for the curve deformation
deps.label: lem:curve-deformation-single-point-estimate

deps.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
deps.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound

deps.title: Piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
deps.label: claim:piecewise-domination-estimate

deps.title: Area bound for immersed curve-shrinking flow in dimension at least 3
deps.label: lem:immersed-curve-shrinking-area-bound

deps.title: Linear bound on the forward difference quotient of area
deps.label: claim:area-forward-difference-linear-bound

deps.title: Approximate piecewise domination by the comparison ODE
deps.label: claim:approximate-piecewise-domination-estimate

entries.label: lem:mu-net-area-comparison
entries.title: Area comparison across a mu-net arc
entries.body:
There is a $\mu>0$ such that the following holds. Let $c,\hat c\in
S^2$. Suppose that there is an arc $\omega$ in $S^2$ connecting $c$
to $\hat c$ with the area of the annulus
$\widetilde\Gamma_\omega^\lambda(t_0)$ in $M\times S^1_\lambda$ less
than $\mu$. Let $v_{\hat c}$, resp., $v_c$, be the solution to
Equation~(\ref{wdiffeqn}) with initial condition $v_{\hat
c}(t_0)=A(\widetilde\Gamma(\hat c))$, resp.,
$v_c(t_0)=A(\widetilde\Gamma(c))$. If
 $$A(p_1\widetilde\Gamma^\lambda_{\hat c}(t_1))\le v_{\hat c}+\zeta/2,$$
then
$$A(p_1(\widetilde\Gamma^\lambda_c(t_1))\le v_c+\zeta.$$

deps.title: Growth rate of the minimal annulus between ramps
deps.label: lem:minimal-annulus-area-growth

entries.label: lem:ramp-length-comparison-small-annulus
entries.title: Length comparison for ramps joined by a small annulus
entries.body:
There is  $\delta>0$ such that for any $r>0$  there is $\bar\mu>0$,
depending on $r$ and on the curvature bound for the ambient Ricci
flow such that the following holds. Suppose that $\gamma$ and $\hat
\gamma$ are ramps in $(M,g(t))\times S^1_\lambda$. Suppose that the
length of $\gamma$ is at least $r$ and suppose that on any
sub-interval $I$ of $\gamma$ of length $r$ we have
$$\int_Ikds<\delta.$$
 Suppose also that there is an annulus connecting $\gamma$ and
$\hat\gamma$ of area less than $\bar\mu$. Then the length of
$\hat\gamma$ is at least $3/4$ the length of $\gamma$.

entries.label: claim:mu-net-length-comparison
entries.title: Length comparison across a mu-net arc
entries.body:
There is $\mu>0$ such that the following holds. Suppose that $c,\hat
c\in S^2$ are such that there is an arc $\omega$ in $S^2$ connecting
$c$ and $\hat c$ such that the area of the annulus
$\widetilde\Gamma^\lambda(\omega)$ is at most $\mu$.  Set
$t_2=t_1-B^{-1}$. If the length of $\widetilde\Gamma_{\hat
c}^\lambda(t)$ is less than $\zeta/2$ for all $t\in [t_2,t_1]$, then
the length of $p_1\widetilde\Gamma_c^\lambda(t_1)$ is less than
$\zeta$.

deps.title: Length comparison for ramps joined by a small annulus
deps.label: lem:ramp-length-comparison-small-annulus

deps.title: Growth rate of the minimal annulus between ramps
deps.label: lem:minimal-annulus-area-growth

deps.title: Sharper single-point estimate for the curve deformation
deps.label: lem:curve-deformation-single-point-estimate

deps.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
deps.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound

deps.title: Area comparison across a mu-net arc
deps.label: lem:mu-net-area-comparison

entries.label: claim:branch-point-metric-deformation
entries.title: Smoothing the metric near interior branch points
entries.body:
Let $\psi\colon A\subset M\times S^1_\lambda$ be an area-minimizing
annulus of area at most $\mu$ with smoothly embedded boundary as
constructed above. Let $h$ be the induced (possibly singular) metric
on $A$ induced by pulling back $g\times ds^2$ by $\psi$, and let
$C''>0$ be an upper bound on the Gaussian curvature of $h$ (away
from the branch points). Then  there is a deformation $\tilde h$ of
$h$, supported near the interior branch points, to a smooth metric
with the property that the area of the deformed smooth metric is at
most $2\mu$ and where the upper bound for the curvature of $\tilde
h$ is $2C''$.

entries.label: prop:annulus-boundary-length-estimate
entries.title: Boundary length estimate for annuli of small area
entries.body:
Fix $0<\delta<1/100$.  For each $0<r$ and $C''<\infty$ there is a
$\mu>0$ such that the following holds. Suppose that $A$ is an
annulus with boundary components $c_0$ and $c_1$. Denote by $l(c_0)$
and $l(c_1)$ the lengths of $c_0$ and $c_1$, respectively. Suppose
that the Gaussian curvature of $A$ is bounded above by $C''$.
Suppose that  $l(c_0)>r$ and that for each sub-interval $I$ of $c_0$
of length $r$, the integral of the absolute value of the geodesic
curvature along $I$ is less than $\delta$. Suppose that the area of
$A$ is less than $\mu$. Then
$$l(c_1)\ge \frac{3}{4}l(c_0).$$

entries.label: rem:high-curvature-set-length-bound
entries.title: Length bound on the high-curvature subset Y
entries.body:
The condition on $k_\mathrm{geod}$ implies that for any arc $J$ in
$c_0$ of length $1$ the total
 length of $J\cap Y$ is less than $\delta$.

entries.label: claim:exponential-map-local-diffeomorphism
entries.title: Exponential map is a local diffeomorphism near c_0
entries.body:
There is $\delta'>0$ such that the following holds.
 The
exponential mapping defines a map $\exp\colon S_X(\delta')\to
A$ which is a local diffeomorphism and the pullback of the metric on
$A$ defines a metric on $S_X(\delta')$ which is at least
$(1-\delta)^2$ times the given product metric.

entries.label: lem:focusing-triangle-base-length-bound
entries.title: Base-length bound for a focusing triangle
entries.body:
Suppose that we have a focusing triangle ${\mathcal T}$ with base $\xi$ bounding a disk $B$
 in $A$.
 Suppose that the length of $\xi$ is at most one.
 Then
$$l(\xi)\le \left(\int_\xi k_\mathrm{geod}ds+\Area(B)\right).$$

deps.title: Angle and curvature bounds for a focusing triangle
deps.label: claim:focusing-triangle-angle-curvature-bound

deps.title: Existence of a shortest geodesic to the focusing-triangle vertex
deps.label: claim:focusing-triangle-vertex-geodesic

entries.label: claim:focusing-triangle-vertex-geodesic
entries.title: Existence of a shortest geodesic to the focusing-triangle vertex
entries.body:
Let $a\in \mathrm{int}\,\xi$. There is a shortest path in $B$ from $a$
to $v$. This shortest path is a geodesic meeting $\partial B$ only
in its end points. It has length $\le(1/2)+\delta'$.

entries.label: claim:focusing-triangle-vertex-geodesic-unique
entries.title: Uniqueness of the minimal geodesic to the vertex
entries.body:
For any $a\in (\partial A\cap B)$ there is a unique minimal geodesic
in $B$ from $a$ to $v$.

entries.label: rem:focusing-triangle-vertex-geodesic-embedded
entries.title: Uniqueness of the embedded geodesic from the vertex
entries.body:
The same argument shows that from any $a\in (\partial A\cap B)$
there is a unique embedded geodesic in $B$ from $v$ to $a$ with
length at most $(1/2)+\delta'$. (Such geodesics may cross more than
once, but the argument given in the lemma applies to sub-geodesics
from $v$ to the first point of intersection along $\gamma$.)

entries.label: claim:arc-length-derivative-bound
entries.title: Derivative bound for the length of the radial family
entries.body: $$\frac{dl}{dt}(t)\le \max_{\psi\in E}h(\psi)\int_{\lambda(t)}k_\mathrm{geod}ds.$$

entries.label: cor:focusing-triangle-base-bound-collection
entries.title: Bound on total base length of a disjoint collection of focusing triangles
entries.body:
Suppose that ${\mathcal T}$ is a focusing triangle with base $\xi$
of length at most one. Then the length of $\xi$ is at most
$\delta+\mu$. More generally, suppose we have a collection of
focusing triangles ${\mathcal T}_1,\ldots,{\mathcal T}_n$ whose
bases all lie in a fixed interval of length one in $c_0$. Suppose
also that the interiors of disks bounded by these focusing triangles
are disjoint. Then the sum of the lengths of the bases is at  most
$\delta+\mu$.

deps.title: Base-length bound for a focusing triangle
deps.label: lem:focusing-triangle-base-length-bound

entries.label: lem:geodesic-no-trivial-embedded-loop
entries.title: No sub-geodesic of D_x is a homotopically trivial embedded loop
entries.body:
For each $x\in X$, there is no non-trivial sub-geodesic of $D_x$
which is a homotopically trivial embedded loop in $A$.

entries.label: lem:no-geodesic-spans-c0-to-c0
entries.title: No D_x spans between two points of c_0
entries.body:
There is no $x$ for which $D_x$ is an embedded arc  with both
endpoints on $c_0$ and otherwise disjoint from $\partial A$.

deps.title: Focusing triangles contain components of J minus X_J and admit limits
deps.label: claim:focusing-triangle-limit-existence

deps.title: Local embedding of the geodesic family near a component of X_J
deps.label: claim:local-embedding-of-geodesic-family

deps.title: Bound on total base length of a disjoint collection of focusing triangles
deps.label: cor:focusing-triangle-base-bound-collection

deps.title: No sub-geodesic of D_x is a homotopically trivial embedded loop
deps.label: lem:geodesic-no-trivial-embedded-loop

deps.title: Length bound on the high-curvature subset Y
deps.label: rem:high-curvature-set-length-bound

entries.label: claim:focusing-triangle-limit-existence
entries.title: Focusing triangles contain components of J minus X_J and admit limits
entries.body:
Any focusing triangle for $J$ must contain a component of $J\setminus X_J $. If
$\{{\mathcal T}_n\}$ is an infinite sequence of focus triangles for $J$, then,
after passing to a subsequence, there is a limiting focusing triangle for $J$.

deps.title: Local embedding of the geodesic family near a component of X_J
deps.label: claim:local-embedding-of-geodesic-family

entries.label: claim:maximal-focusing-triangles-disjoint
entries.title: Disjointness of successive maximal focusing triangles
entries.body:
The interior of ${\mathcal T}_2$ is disjoint from the interior of
${\mathcal T}_1$.

entries.label: def:maximal-focusing-triangle-set
entries.title: Maximal set of focusing triangles
entries.body:
We call the triangles ${\mathcal T}_1,\ldots,{\mathcal T}_k$
constructed above, the {\em maximal set of focusing triangles} for
$J$ relative to $B$.

entries.label: claim:remaining-set-length-bound
entries.title: Length bound on the complement of the focusing-triangle bases
entries.body: The length of $X'_J$ is at least $1-2\delta-\mu$.

deps.title: Bound on total base length of a disjoint collection of focusing triangles
deps.label: cor:focusing-triangle-base-bound-collection

deps.title: Length bound on the high-curvature subset Y
deps.label: rem:high-curvature-set-length-bound

entries.label: claim:exponential-map-embedding-on-remainder
entries.title: Embedding of the exponential map on the remaining set
entries.body:
The restriction of the exponential mapping to $S_{X'_J}(B)$ is an
embedding.

entries.label: lem:geodesic-no-embedded-loop-general
entries.title: No sub-geodesic of D_x is an embedded loop in A
entries.body:
For any $x\in X$ there is no sub-geodesic of $D_x$ that is an
embedded loop in $A$.

deps.title: No D_x spans between two points of c_0
deps.label: lem:no-geodesic-spans-c0-to-c0

entries.label: lem:far-apart-geodesics-disjoint
entries.title: Geodesics D_x, D_y from far-apart points do not meet
entries.body:
Let $x,y$ be distinct points of $X$. Suppose that there are
sub-geodesics $D'_x\subset D_x$ and $D'_y\subset D_y$ with a common
endpoint. Then the arc $D'_x*(D'_y)^{-1}$ cuts $A$ into two
complementary components, exactly one of which is a disk, denoted
$B$. Then it is not possible for  $B\cap c_0$ to contain an arc of
length $1$.

deps.title: No D_x spans between two points of c_0
deps.label: lem:no-geodesic-spans-c0-to-c0

entries.label: cor:focusing-triangle-from-short-arc
entries.title: Short arcs with meeting geodesics bound a focusing disk
entries.body:
Suppose that we have an arc $\xi$ of length at most $1$ on $c_0$.
Denote the endpoints of $\xi$ by $x$ and $y$ and  suppose that
$D_x\cap D_y\not=\emptyset$. Let $D'_x$ and $D'_y$ be sub-geodesics
containing $x$ and $y$ respectively ending at the same point, $v$,
and otherwise disjoint. Then the loop $\xi*D'_y*(D'_x)^{-1}$ bounds
a disk in $A$.

deps.title: No D_x spans between two points of c_0
deps.label: lem:no-geodesic-spans-c0-to-c0

entries.label: cor:geodesic-family-intersection-summary
entries.title: Summary of intersection properties of the geodesics D_x
entries.body:
For each $x\in X$, the geodesic $D_x$ is an embedded arc in $A$.
Either it has length $\delta'$ or its final point lies on $c_1$.
Suppose there are   $x\not=x'$ in $X$ with $D_x\cap
D_{x'}\not=\emptyset$. Then there is an arc $\xi$ on $c_0$
connecting $x$ to $x'$ with the length of $\xi$ at most
$\delta+\mu$. Furthermore, for sub-geodesics $D'_x\subset D_x$,
containing $x$, and $D'_{x'}\subset D_{x'}$, containing $x'$, that
intersect exactly in an endpoint of each, the loop
$\xi*D'_{x'}*(D'_x)^{-1}$ bounds a disk $B$ in $A$, and the length
of $\xi$ is at most the turning of $\xi$ plus the area of $B$.

deps.title: No D_x spans between two points of c_0
deps.label: lem:no-geodesic-spans-c0-to-c0

deps.title: Short arcs with meeting geodesics bound a focusing disk
deps.label: cor:focusing-triangle-from-short-arc

deps.title: Boundary length estimate for annuli of small area
deps.label: prop:annulus-boundary-length-estimate

entries.label: claim:focusing-triangle-total-base-length-bound
entries.title: Total base length of focusing triangles meeting a unit interval
entries.body:
For every subinterval $J$ of length one in $c_0$ the total length of
the bases $\xi_i$ that meet $J$ is at most $2\delta+\mu<0.03$.

deps.title: Bound on total base length of a disjoint collection of focusing triangles
deps.label: cor:focusing-triangle-base-bound-collection

entries.label: lem:curve-shrinking-shi-estimate-first-inequality
entries.title: First curvature bound in the curve-shrinking Shi estimate
entries.body:
Let $(W,h(t)),\ t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a Ricci flow and fix
$\Theta<\infty$. Then there exist constants $\delta>0$ and $0<r_0\le
1$   depending only on the curvature bound for the ambient Ricci
flow and $\Theta$ such that the following holds. Let $c(x,t),\
t_0\le t\le t_1$, be a curve-shrinking flow with $c(\cdot,t)$
immersed for each $t\in [t_0,t_1]$ and with the total curvature of
$c(\cdot,t_0)$ being at most $\Theta$. Suppose that there is $0<r\le
r_0$ and at a time $t'\in [t_0,t_1-\delta r^2]$ such that the length
of $c(\cdot,t')$ is at least $r$ and the total curvature of
$c(\cdot,t')$ on any sub-arc of length $r$ is at most $\delta$. Then
for every $t\in [t',t'+\delta r^2]$ the curvature $k$ satisfies
$$k^2\le  \frac{2}{(t-t')}.$$

entries.label: claim:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-uniform-bound
entries.title: Uniform bound on length and total curvature on short sub-arcs
entries.body:
There is a constant $D_0<\infty$, depending only on $\Theta$ and the
curvature bound of the ambient Ricci flow such that for every $t\in
[t',t'+\delta r^2]$ and every sub-arc $\gamma_{t'}$ whose length is
at most $r$, we have $\int_{\gamma_t} kds<D_0$ and $l(\gamma_t)\le
D_0r$, where $l(\gamma_t)$ is the length of $\gamma_t$.

deps.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
deps.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound

entries.label: claim:cutoff-function-time-derivative-bound
entries.title: Bound on the time derivative of the cutoff function
entries.body:
There is a constant $D_1$ depending only on the curvature bound for
 the ambient Ricci flow such that for any sub-arc $\gamma_{t'}$ of
 length $r$, defining $\varphi(x,t)$ as above, for all $x\in \gamma_t$
 and all $t\in [t',t_2]$ we have
$$\left|\frac{\partial \varphi(x,t)}{\partial t}\right|\le
\frac{D_1}{r\sqrt{t-t'}}+D_1.$$

deps.title: Uniform bound on length and total curvature on short sub-arcs
deps.label: claim:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-uniform-bound

deps.title: First variation formulas for curve-shrinking flow
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-first-variation

entries.label: claim:cutoff-weighted-curvature-integral-bound
entries.title: Bound on the cutoff-weighted curvature integral
entries.body:
There is a constant $D_2$ depending only on the curvature bound of
the ambient Ricci flow and $\Theta$ and a constant $D_3$ depending
only on the curvature bound of the ambient Ricci flow, such that for
any $t\in [t',t_2]$ and any sub-arc $\gamma_{t'}$ of length $r$, we
have
$$\left|\frac{d}{dt}\int_{\gamma_t}\varphi
kds\right|\le
D_2\left(1+\frac{1}{r\sqrt{t-t'}}\right)+\frac{D_2}{r^2}+D_3\int_{\gamma_t}\varphi
kds.$$

deps.title: Growth bounds for length and total curvature
deps.label: lem:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-growth

deps.title: Uniform bound on length and total curvature on short sub-arcs
deps.label: claim:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-uniform-bound

deps.title: Bound on the time derivative of the cutoff function
deps.label: claim:cutoff-function-time-derivative-bound

entries.label: cor:cutoff-weighted-curvature-integral-sqrt-bound
entries.title: Square-root bound on the cutoff-weighted curvature integral
entries.body:
For any $t\in [t',t_2]$  and any sub-arc $\gamma_{t'}$ of length $r$
we have
$$\int_{\gamma_t}\varphi kds
\le  D_4\sqrt{\delta}$$ for a constant $D_4$ that depends only on
the sectional curvature bound of the ambient Ricci flow and
$\Theta$.

entries.label: cor:curvature-integral-uniform-bound
entries.title: Uniform bound on the curvature integral over short sub-arcs
entries.body:
For $\gamma_{t'}\subset c(\cdot,t')$ a sub-arc of length at most $r$
 and for any $t\in [t',t_2]$, we have
$$\int_{\gamma_t}kds\le
2D_4\sqrt{\delta}.$$ For any $t\in [t',t_2]$ and any sub-arc
$J\subset c(\cdot,t)$ of length at most $r/2$ with respect to the
metric $h(t)$, we have
$$\int_Jk(x,t)ds(x,t)\le 2D_4\sqrt{\delta}.$$

deps.title: Exponential bound for length and total curvature
deps.label: cor:curve-shrinking-length-curvature-exponential-bound

entries.label: claim:graph-flow-velocity-comparison
entries.title: Comparison of the graph-flow and curve-shrinking velocities

entries.label: claim:graph-flow-angle-and-norm-bounds
entries.title: Angle and norm bounds for the graph flow
entries.body:
Provided that $\delta>0$ is sufficiently small,
the angle (measured in $h(t)$) between $Y$ and $Z=(1,f_z)$ is
greater than $\pi/4$. Also,
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $$k\le |Y|<\sqrt{2}k.$$
\item[(2)] $$\left|\langle \nabla_ZZ,Z\rangle\right|<(k+ 2\delta) |Z|^3.$$
\item[(3)] $$\left|\nabla_ZZ\right|<2(|Y|+\delta).$$
\item[(4)] $$|\langle Y,Z\rangle |\le |Y||f_z|(1+3\delta).$$
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: claim:graph-flow-connection-term-bounds
entries.title: Bounds for the connection correction term
entries.body:
The following hold provided that $\delta>0$ is sufficiently small:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $|Z(\psi(Z))|<C'(1+\delta |Y|)$, and
\item[(2)] $|Y(\psi(Z))|<C'(|Y|+\delta|\nabla_ZY|)$.
\end{enumerate}
(Recall that $C'$ is a constant depending only on the curvature bound of the
ambient Ricci flow.)

entries.label: claim:graph-flow-mixed-derivative-bound
entries.title: Mixed derivative bound for the graph flow
entries.body: $$|\langle \nabla_ZY,Z\rangle|\le (|f_z|(|\nabla_ZY|+2\delta|Y|)+\delta|Z|^2|Y|)(1+\delta).$$

entries.label: lem:neck-shortening-curve
entries.title: Shortening a curve through the negative end of a neck
entries.body:
The following holds for all $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small. Suppose
that $(M,g)$ is a Riemannian manifold and that $N\subset M$ is an
$\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$. Let $S(x)$ be the central
two-sphere of this neck and suppose that $S(x)$ separates $M$. Let
$y\in M$. Orient $s$ so that $y$ lies in the closure of the positive
side of $S(x)$. Let $\gamma\colon [0,a]\to M$ be a rectifiable curve
from $x$ to $y$. If $\gamma$ contains a point of
$s^{-1}(-\epsilon^{-1},-\epsilon^{-1}/2)$ then there is a
rectifiable curve from $x$ to $y$ contained in the closure of the
positive side of $S(x)$ whose length is at most the length of
$\gamma$ minus $\frac{1}{2}\epsilon^{-1}R(x)^{-1/2}$.

entries.label: lem:epsilon-neck-curvature-pinching
entries.title: Curvature pinching in an $\epsilon$-neck
entries.body:
For any $0<\alpha<1/8$  there is $\epsilon_1=\epsilon_1(\alpha)>0$ such that
the following two conditions hold for all $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_1$.
\begin{enumerate} \item[(1)] If $(N,g)$ is an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$ of
scale one (i.e., with $R(x)=1$) then the principal sectional
curvatures at any point of $N$ are within $\alpha/6$ of
$\{1/2,0,0\}$. In particular, for any $y\in N$ we have
$$(1-\alpha)\le R(y)\le (1+\alpha).$$
\item[(2)] There is unique two-plane of maximal sectional curvature at
every point of an $\epsilon$-neck, and the angle between the
distribution of two-planes of maximal sectional curvature and the
two-plane field tangent to the family of two-spheres of the
$\epsilon$-neck structure is everywhere less than $\alpha$.
\end{enumerate}

entries.label: cor:embedded-sphere-isotopic-to-neck-fiber
entries.title: An almost-round embedded sphere is isotopic to a neck fiber
entries.body:
The following holds for any $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small. Suppose
that $(N,g)$ is an $\epsilon$-neck and we have  and an embedding
$f\colon S^2 \to N$ with the property that the restriction of $g$ to
the image of this embedding is within $\epsilon$ in the
$C^{[1/\epsilon]}$-topology to the round metric $h_0$ of scalar
curvature one on $S^2$ and with the norm of the second fundamental
form less than $\epsilon$. Then the two-sphere $f(S^2)$ is isotopic
in $N$ to any member of the family of two-spheres coming from the
$\epsilon$-neck structure on $N$.

entries.label: lem:neck-geodesic-directions
entries.title: Minimal geodesics in a neck nearly align with the axis direction
entries.body:
For any $\alpha>0$  there is $\epsilon_2=\epsilon_2(\alpha)>0$
such that the following hold for all $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_2$.
Suppose that $(N,g)$ is an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$ and
$R(x)=1$. Suppose that $\gamma$ is a minimal geodesic in  $N$ from
$p$ to $q$. We suppose that $\gamma$ is parameterized by arc length,
is of length $\ell>\epsilon^{-1}/100$, and that $s(p)<s(q)$. Then
for all $s$ in the domain of definition of $\gamma$ we have
$$|\gamma'(s)-(\partial/\partial s)|_g<\alpha.$$
 In particular, the angle between $\gamma'$ and $\partial/\partial
s$ is less than $2\alpha$.
 Also,  any
member $S^2$ of the family of two-spheres in the $N$ has intrinsic
diameter at most $(1+\alpha)\sqrt{2}\pi$.

entries.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates
entries.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
entries.body:
For any $\alpha>0$ there is $\epsilon_3=\epsilon_3(\alpha)>0$ such
that the  following hold for any $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_3$ and any
$\epsilon$-neck $N$ of scale $1$ centered at $x$.
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] Suppose that $p$ and $q$ are points of  $N$
with either $|s(q)-s(p)|\ge \epsilon^{-1}/100$ or $d(p,q)\ge
\epsilon^{-1}/100$. Then  we have
$$(1-\alpha)|s(q)-s(p)|\le d(p,q)\le (1+\alpha)|s(q)-s(p)|.$$
\item[(2)]
$$B(x,(1-\alpha)\epsilon^{-1})\subset
N\subset B(x,(1+\alpha)\epsilon^{-1}).$$
\item[(3)] Any geodesic that exits from both ends of $N$ has length at least $2(1-\alpha)
\epsilon^{-1}$.\end{enumerate}

entries.label: cor:shortest-geodesic-crosses-neck-once
entries.title: A long shortest geodesic crosses each neck sphere at most once
entries.body:
The following holds for all $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small. Let $N$
be an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$. If $\gamma$ is a shortest
geodesic in $N$ between its endpoints and if
$|\gamma|>R(x)^{-1/2}\epsilon^{-1}/100$, then $\gamma$ crosses each
two-sphere in the neck structure on $N$ at most once.

entries.label: lem:geodesic-crosses-neck-once-general
entries.title: A shortest geodesic crossing a neck at most once, in general
entries.body:
The following holds for every $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small.
Suppose that $(M,g)$ is a Riemannian manifold and that $N\subset M$
is an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$ and suppose that $\gamma$ is a
shortest geodesic in $M$ between its endpoints and that the length
of every component of $N\cap|\gamma|$ has length at least $
R(x)^{-1/2}\epsilon^{-1}/8$. Then $\gamma$ crosses each two-sphere
in the neck structure on $N$ at most once.

deps.title: A long shortest geodesic crosses each neck sphere at most once
deps.label: cor:shortest-geodesic-crosses-neck-once

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

deps.title: Minimal geodesics in a neck nearly align with the axis direction
deps.label: lem:neck-geodesic-directions

entries.label: cor:minimal-geodesic-through-core
entries.title: A minimal geodesic through the core of a cap
entries.body:
The following holds for all $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small and any
$C<\infty$. Let $X$ be an $(C,\epsilon)$-cap in a complete
Riemannian manifold $(M,g)$, and let $Y$ be its core and let $S$ be
the central two-sphere of the $\epsilon$-neck $N=X-\overline Y$. We
orient the $s$-direction in $N$ so that $Y$ lies off the negative
end of $N$. Let $\widehat Y$ be the union of $\overline Y$ and the
closed negative half of $N$ and let $S$ be the boundary of $\widehat
Y$. Suppose that $\gamma$ is a minimal geodesic in $(M,g)$ that
contains a point of the core $Y$. Then the intersection of $\gamma$
with $\widehat Y$ is an interval containing an endpoint of $\gamma$.

deps.title: A long shortest geodesic crosses each neck sphere at most once
deps.label: cor:shortest-geodesic-crosses-neck-once

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

entries.label: lem:neck-distance-comparison-external-point
entries.title: Distance comparison from a point outside the neck
entries.body:
Given $0<\alpha<1$ there is $\epsilon_4=\epsilon_4(\alpha)>0$ such
that the following holds for any $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_4$. Suppose
that $N$ is an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$ in a connected
manifold $M$ (here we are not assuming that $R(x)=1$). We suppose
that the central $2$-sphere of $N$ separates $M$. Let $z$ be a point
outside of  the middle two-thirds of $N$ and lying on the negative
side of the central $2$-sphere of $N$. (We allow both the case when
$z\in N$ and when $z\not\in N$.) Let $p$ be a point in the middle
half of $N$. Let $\mu\colon [0,a]\to N$ be a straight line segment
(with respect to the standard product metric) in the positive
$s$-direction in $N$ beginning at $p$ and ending at a point $q$ of
$N$. Then
$$(1-\alpha)(s(q)-s(p))\le d(z,q)-d(z,p)\le (1+\alpha)(s(q)-s(p)).$$

entries.label: lem:metric-sphere-meets-neck-topologically
entries.title: Metric-ball boundary meets a neck in a topological sphere
entries.body:
Given any $\alpha>0$  there is $\epsilon(\alpha)>0$ such that  the
following holds for any $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon(\alpha)$. Suppose
that $N$ is an $\epsilon$-neck centered at $x$ in a connected
manifold $M$ (here we are not assuming that $R(x)=1$) and that $z$
is a point outside the middle two-thirds of $N$. We suppose that the
central two-sphere of $N$ separates $M$. Let $p$ be a point in the
middle sixth of $N$ at distance $d$ from $z$. Then the intersection
of the boundary of the metric ball $B(z,d)$ with $N$ is a
topological $2$-sphere contained in the middle quarter of $N$ that
maps homeomorphically onto $S^2$ under the projection mapping $N\to
S^2$ determined by the $\epsilon$-neck structure. Furthermore, if
$p'\in \partial B(z,d)$ then $|s(p)-s(p')|<\alpha
R(x)^{-1/2}\epsilon^{-1}$.

deps.title: Distance comparison from a point outside the neck
deps.label: lem:neck-distance-comparison-external-point

entries.label: prop:overlapping-necks-properties
entries.title: Properties of overlapping $\epsilon$-necks
entries.body:
Given $0<\alpha\le 10^{-2}$, there is
$\epsilon_5=\epsilon_5(\alpha)>0$ such that the following hold for
all $0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_5$. Let $N$ and $N'$ be $\epsilon$-necks
centered at $x$ and $x'$, respectively, in a Riemannian manifold
$X$:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] If $N\cap N'\not=\emptyset$ then
$1-\alpha<R(x)/R(x')<1+\alpha$. In particular, denoting the scales
of $N$ and $N'$ by $h$ and $h'$ we have
$$1-\alpha<\frac{h}{h'}<1+\alpha.$$
\item[(2)] Suppose $y\in N\cap N'$ and $S$ and $S'$ are the two-spheres in the $\epsilon$-neck
structures on $N$ and $N'$, respectively, passing through $y$. Then
the angle between $TS_y$ and $TS'_y$ is less than $\alpha$.
\item[(3)] Suppose that $y\in N\cap N'$. Denote by $\partial/\partial s_N$ and $\partial/\partial s_{N'}$
the tangent vectors in the $\epsilon$-neck structures of $N$ and
$N'$, respectively. Then at the point $y$, either
$$|R(x)^{1/2}(\partial /\partial s_N)-R(x')^{1/2}\partial/\partial s_{N'}|<\alpha$$
or
$$|R(x)^{1/2}(\partial /\partial s_N)+R(x')^{1/2}\partial/\partial s_{N'}|<\alpha.$$
 \item[(4)] Suppose that
one of the two-spheres $S'$ of the $\epsilon$-neck structure on $N'$
is completely contained in $N$. Then $S'$ is a section of the
projection mapping on the first factor
$$p_1\colon S^2\times(-\epsilon^{-1},\epsilon^{-1})\to S^2.$$
In particular, $S'$ is isotopic in $N$ to any one of the two-spheres
of the $\epsilon$-neck structure on $N$ by an isotopy that moves all
points in the interval directions.
\item[(5)] If $N\cap N'$ contains a point $y$ with $(-0.9)\epsilon^{-1}\le s_N(y)\le
(0.9)\epsilon^{-1}$, then there is a point $y'\in N\cap N'$ such
that
$$-(0.96)\epsilon^{-1}\le s_N(y')\le (0.96)\epsilon^{-1}$$
$$-(0.96)\epsilon^{-1}\le s_{N'}(y')\le (0.96)\epsilon^{-1}.$$
The two-sphere $S(y')$ in the neck structure on $N$ through $y'$ is contained
in $N'$ and the two-sphere $S'(y')$ in the neck structure on $N'$ through $y'$
is contained in $N$. Furthermore, $S(y')$ and $S'(y')$ are isotopic in $N\cap
N'$. Lastly, $N\cap N'$ is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times (0,1)$ under a
diffeomorphism mapping $S(y)$ to $S^2\times \{1/2\}$.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: An almost-round embedded sphere is isotopic to a neck fiber
deps.label: cor:embedded-sphere-isotopic-to-neck-fiber

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

deps.title: Minimal geodesics in a neck nearly align with the axis direction
deps.label: lem:neck-geodesic-directions

entries.label: def:chain-of-epsilon-necks
entries.title: Finite and infinite chains of $\epsilon$-necks
entries.body:
Let $(X,g)$ be a Riemannian manifold. By a {\em finite chain} of
$\epsilon$-necks in $(X,g)$, we mean a sequence $N_a,\ldots,N_{b}$,
of $\epsilon$-necks in $(X,g)$ such that: \begin{enumerate} \item
for all $i,\ a\le i<b$, the intersection $N_i\cap N_{i+1}$ contains
the positive-most quarter of $N_i$ and the negative-most quarter of
$N_{i+1}$ and is contained in the positive-most three-quarters of
$N_i$ and the negative-most three-quarters of $N_{i+1}$, and
\item  for all $i,\ a<i\le b$, $N_i$ is disjoint from the negative end of
$N_a$.
\end{enumerate}
By an {\em infinite chain} of $\epsilon$-necks in $X$ we mean a
collection $\{N_i\}_{i\in I}$ for some interval $I\subset \Zee$,
infinite in at least one direction, so that for each finite
subinterval $J$ of $I$ the subset of $\{N_i\}_{i\in J}$ is a chain
of $\epsilon$-necks.

entries.label: lem:chain-union-diffeomorphic-cylinder
entries.title: Union of a chain of necks is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times(0,1)$
entries.body:
The union $U$ of the $N_i$ in a finite or infinite chain of
$\epsilon$-necks is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times (0,1)$. In
particular, it is an $\epsilon$-tube.

entries.label: cor:chain-frontier
entries.title: Frontier of a finite chain of necks
entries.body:
Let $\{N_a,\ldots,N_b\}$ be a chain of $\epsilon$-necks. If a
connected set $Y$ meets both $U=\cup_{a\le i\le b}N_i$ and its
complement, then $Y$ either contains  points of the frontier of the
negative end $N_a$ or of the positive  end of $N_b$.

entries.label: lem:infinite-chain-frontier
entries.title: Frontier of an infinite chain of necks
entries.body:
Suppose that $\{N_0,\cdots\}$ is an infinite chain of
$\epsilon$-necks in $M$. Then the frontier of $U=\cup_{i=0}^\infty
N_i$ is the frontier of the negative end of $N_0$.

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

entries.label: lem:chain-fibration-by-spheres
entries.title: Geometric fibration of a neck chain by two-spheres
entries.body:
There is $\epsilon_0>0$ such that  the following holds for all
$0<\epsilon\le \epsilon_0$. Suppose that $\{N_j\}_{j\in J}$ is a
chain of $\epsilon$-necks in a Riemannian manifold $M$. Let
$U=\cup_{j\in J}N_j$. Then there exist an interval $I$ and a smooth
map $p\colon U\to I$ such that every fiber of $p$ is a two-sphere,
and if $y$ is in the middle $7/8$'s of $N_j$ then the fiber
$p^{-1}(p(y))$ makes a small angle at every point with the family of
two-spheres in the $\epsilon$-neck $N_j$.

entries.label: lem:two-necks-form-balanced-chain
entries.title: Two adjacent necks form a balanced chain
entries.body:
There exists $\epsilon_0>0$ such that for all $0<\epsilon\le
\epsilon_0$ the following is true.  Suppose that $N$ and $N'$ are
$\epsilon$-necks centered at $x$ and $x'$, respectively, in a
Riemannian manifold $M$. Suppose that $x'$ is not contained in $N$
but is contained in the closure of $N$ in $M$. Suppose also that the
two-spheres of the neck structure on $N$ and $N'$ separate $M$.
Then, possibly after reversing the $\epsilon$-neck structures on $N$
and/or $N'$, the pair $\{N,N'\}$ forms a balanced chain.

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

entries.label: lem:extend-balanced-chain
entries.title: Extending a balanced chain by one neck
entries.body:
There exists $\epsilon_0>0$ such that for all $0<\epsilon\le
\epsilon_0$ the following is true.  Suppose that
$\{N_a,\ldots,N_b\}$ is a balanced chain in a Riemannian manifold
$M$ with $U=\cup_{i=a}^bN_i$. Suppose that the two-spheres of the
neck structure of $N_a$ separate $M$.
 Suppose that $x$
is a point of the frontier of $U$ contained in the closure of the
plus end of $N_b$ that is also the center of an $\epsilon$-neck $N$.
Then possibly after reversing the direction of $N$, we have that
$\{N_a,\ldots,N_b,N\}$ is a balanced chain. Similarly, if $x$ is in
the closure of the minus end of $N_a$, then (again after possibly
reversing the direction of $N$) we have that $\{N,N_a,\ldots,N_b\}$
is a balanced $\epsilon$-chain.

deps.title: Two adjacent necks form a balanced chain
deps.label: lem:two-necks-form-balanced-chain

entries.label: prop:balanced-chain-covers-connected-set
entries.title: A connected set of neck centers is covered by a balanced chain
entries.body:
There exists $\epsilon_0>0$ such that for all $0<\epsilon\le
\epsilon_0$ the following is true. Let $X$ be a connected subset of
a Riemannian manifold $M$ with the property that every point $x\in
X$ is the center of an $\epsilon$-neck $N(x)$ in $M$. Suppose that
the central two-spheres of these necks do not separate $M$. Then
there is a subset $\{x_i\}$ of $X$ such that the necks $N(x_i)$
(possibly after reversing their $s$-directions) form a balanced
chain of $\epsilon$-necks $\{N(x_i)\}$ whose union $U$ contains $X$.
The union $U$ is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times (0,1)$. It is an
$\epsilon$-tube.

deps.title: Union of a chain of necks is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times(0,1)$
deps.label: lem:chain-union-diffeomorphic-cylinder

deps.title: Extending a balanced chain by one neck
deps.label: lem:extend-balanced-chain

deps.title: Frontier of an infinite chain of necks
deps.label: lem:infinite-chain-frontier

entries.label: lem:manifold-covered-by-necks-classification
entries.title: A manifold covered by $\epsilon$-necks is a tube or an $S^2$-fibration over $S^1$
entries.body:
The following holds for every $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small. Let
$(M,g)$ be a connected Riemannian manifold. Suppose that every point
of $M$ is the center of an $\epsilon$-neck. Then either $M$ is
diffeomorphic to $S^2\times (0,1)$ and is an $\epsilon$-tube, or $M$
is diffeomorphic to an $S^2$-fibration over $S^1$.

deps.title: A connected set of neck centers is covered by a balanced chain
deps.label: prop:balanced-chain-covers-connected-set

entries.label: prop:connected-set-in-caps-and-necks
entries.title: Connected sets covered by caps and necks
entries.body:
For any $C<\infty$ the following holds. Suppose that $X$ is a
connected subset of a Riemannian three-manifold $(M,g)$. Suppose
that every point of $X$ is either the center of an $\epsilon$-neck
or is contained in the core of a $(C,\epsilon)$-cap. Then one of the
following holds:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $X$ is contained in a component of $M$ that is the union of
two $(C,\epsilon)$-caps. This component is diffeomorphic to $S^3$,
$\Ar P^3$ or $\Ar P^3\#\Ar P^3$.
\item[(2)] $X$ is contained in a component of $M$ that is a double $C$-capped $\epsilon$-tube.
 This component is diffeomorphic to $S^3$, $\Ar P^3$ or $\Ar
P^3\#\Ar P^3$.
\item[(3)] $X$ is contained in a single $(C,\epsilon)$-cap.
\item[(4)] $X$ is contained in a $C$-capped $\epsilon$-tube.
\item[(5)] $X$ is contained in an $\epsilon$-tube.
\item[(6)] $X$ is contained in a component of $M$ that is an
$\epsilon$-fibration, which itself is a union of $\epsilon$-necks.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

deps.title: A manifold covered by $\epsilon$-necks is a tube or an $S^2$-fibration over $S^1$
deps.label: lem:manifold-covered-by-necks-classification

deps.title: A connected set of neck centers is covered by a balanced chain
deps.label: prop:balanced-chain-covers-connected-set

deps.title: Frontier of an infinite chain of necks
deps.label: lem:infinite-chain-frontier

entries.label: claim:cap-union-is-component
entries.title: Adjacent cap union is a component of $M$
entries.body:
If $C'$ is a $(C,\epsilon)$-cap whose core contains a point of the
frontier of $\widetilde C$, then $\widetilde C\cup C'$ is a
component of $M$ containing $X$.

deps.title: Distance and diameter estimates in an $\epsilon$-neck
deps.label: cor:neck-distance-estimates

deps.title: An isotopic separating sphere yields a component of $M$
deps.label: claim:isotopic-sphere-gives-component

entries.label: prop:classification-covered-by-necks-caps
entries.title: Topological classification of manifolds covered by necks and caps
entries.body:
For all $\epsilon>0$ sufficiently small the following holds. Suppose
that $(M,g)$ is a connected Riemannian manifold such that every
point is either contained in the core of a $(C,\epsilon)$-cap in $M$
or is the center of an $\epsilon$-neck in $M$. Then one of the
following holds:
\begin{enumerate}
\item[(1)] $M$ is diffeomorphic to $S^3$, $\Ar P^3$ or $\Ar P^3\#\Ar
P^3$,
and $M$ is either a double $C$-capped $\epsilon$-tube or is the
union of two $(C,\epsilon)$-caps.
\item[(2)] $M$ is diffeomorphic to  $\Ar^3$ or $\Ar P^3\setminus \{\mathrm{point}\}$,
and $M$ is either a $(C,\epsilon$-cap or a $C$-capped
$\epsilon$-tube.
\item[(3)] $M$ is diffeomorphic to $S^2\times \Ar$ and is an
$\epsilon$-tube.
\item[(4)] $M$ is diffeomorphic to an $S^2$-bundle over $S^1$ and is an $\epsilon$-fibration.
\end{enumerate}

deps.title: Connected sets covered by caps and necks
deps.label: prop:connected-set-in-caps-and-necks
